cxo_profile.name: K Krithivasan
cxo_profile.title: Chief Executive Officer
cxo_profile.role_focus: Becoming the world's largest AI-led services company while defending industry-leading margins and building the largest AI-ready workforce
cxo_profile.priorities: GenAI revenue ramp, large-deal TCV growth, margin defense, attrition control & reskilling

attention_cards.title: Accenture GenAI bookings hit $3B, pulling ahead in TCS's target segment
attention_cards.body: Accenture reported $3B in GenAI bookings this quarter vs TCS's $1.5B ramp. Three of TCS's top-20 accounts issued GenAI RFPs Accenture is contesting.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: How do we close the GenAI bookings gap with Accenture?

investigation.seed_question: What is the Accenture GenAI threat and how should we counter it?
investigation.context_summary: Accenture GenAI · $3B bookings · consulting-led motion · 3 TCS top-20 accounts contested
investigation.recommended_action: Stand up a GenAI deal SWAT team for the 3 contested accounts within 5 business days. Introduce an outcome-based GenAI SKU for mid-market. CFO co-sign on pricing floor.

panels.label: Why we believe this
panels.title: Why we believe this — Evidence view
panels.subtitle: Sources and correlation

payload.heading: What triggered this
payload.body:
• **Accenture Q2 earnings call:** reported $3B GenAI bookings, up from $1.8B QoQ, explicitly citing 'consulting-led reinvention' wins in BFSI and Products.
• **Field intel (3 accounts):** Global Bank A, Insurer B and Telco D each issued GenAI transformation RFPs in the last 30 days; Accenture is incumbent advisor on two.
• **Internal CRM:** TCS GenAI pipeline coverage in these accounts is 3.1x but win-rate has slipped 6pp QoQ as deals stall in evaluation.

payload.heading: Why it matters now
payload.body: The 3 contested accounts represent $2.1B of at-risk pipeline. Accenture's consulting-first motion converts ~18 days faster; every stalled evaluation cycle widens the bookings gap. A 5-day RFP response window is closing on Global Bank A.

payload.heading: Contested accounts
payload.body:
• **Global Bank A** ($420M ARR): GenAI fraud + ops RFP, 5-day response window, Accenture incumbent advisor.
• **Insurer B** ($180M ARR): claims automation GenAI pilot expanding to enterprise; TCS + Accenture shortlisted.
• **Telco D** ($260M ARR): network ops GenAI RFP; decision expected in 3 weeks.

panels.label: Stage the GenAI SWAT response
panels.title: GenAI SWAT Team — Competitive Counter Staging
panels.subtitle: 48-hour mobilization

payload.heading: Recommended staging
payload.body:
• **SWAT pod per account:** 1 partner + 1 GenAI solution architect + 1 domain SME. Route within 48 hrs.
• **Outcome-based proposal:** lead with fixed-outcome pricing on the first GenAI use case to neutralize Accenture's consulting framing.
• **Reference velocity:** deploy the 2 closed BFSI GenAI references into Global Bank A within 5 days.

payload.heading: Recommended guardrail adjustments
payload.body:
• **Outcome-based floor:** set minimum blended margin at 24% on GenAI outcome deals. CFO co-sign required.
• **Mid-market SKU:** introduce a productized GenAI bundle 15% below custom to counter Infosys Topaz, capped at 3 use cases.
• **Discount ceiling:** hold at 18% for GenAI; no exceptions without VP Revenue Mgmt sign-off.

panels.label: Invite team to investigate
panels.title: Invite team to investigate — Working Group
panels.subtitle: Collaborate on the response

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: Loop in Head of GenAI, VP BFSI Sales, and the CFO office for the pricing floor sign-off. Kick off a 48-hour war-room on the 3 contested accounts.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Confirm SWAT pods staffed.
• Send outcome-based proposal draft to Global Bank A.
• Pull the 2 BFSI GenAI references.

attention_cards.title: Q2 operating margin tracking to 24.3% — 170bps below aspiration as the wage cycle lands
attention_cards.body: The Q1 wage revision and GenAI capability investment are compressing margin ahead of the H2 utilization recovery. Q2 is the inflection that sets the FY26 margin narrative with the Street.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: Which levers protect Q2 margin without cutting GenAI investment?

investigation.seed_question: How do we defend Q2 margin while protecting the AI-led investment agenda?
investigation.context_summary: Margin 24.3% · wage cycle -110bps · GenAI invest -60bps · utilization recovering · FY band 26-28%
investigation.recommended_action: Approve the Q2 margin-defense mix — utilization +1.5pp, pyramid rebalancing (fresher intake), subcontractor -8%, discretionary-cost hold — while ring-fencing the GenAI capability budget. CFO Samir Seksaria co-owns the plan.

affected_accounts.name: North America onshore mix — delivery cost

payload.heading: What triggered this
payload.body:
• **Annual wage revision** landed in Q1, the single largest quarterly drag at ~110bps — standard for the cycle but front-loaded.
• **GenAI capability investment** (tcsAI, platforms, reskilling) is a deliberate ~60bps drag — capability ahead of revenue.
• **FX and furlough tail** from Q4 added a residual few bps. None of these are structural.

payload.heading: Why it matters now
payload.body: Q2 is the swing quarter. If margin recovers toward the 26% zone, the FY26 narrative holds and the AI-investment thesis is validated. If it stays sub-25%, the Street questions whether GenAI spend is diluting the industry-leading margin.

payload.heading: Recommended lever mix
payload.body:
• **Utilization +1.5pp:** redeploy bench into the healthy order book (book-to-bill 1.1x); the fastest, lowest-risk lever.
• **Pyramid rebalancing:** lift fresher mix via the 42,000 FY26 trainee intake to reset the cost curve.
• **Subcontractor -8%:** substitute subcontractor spend with reskilled internal talent as AI-fluency scales.
• **Discretionary-cost hold:** freeze non-essential discretionary spend for two quarters — no hiring freeze on hot skills.

payload.heading: What must stay funded
payload.body: The margin defense must **not** come from the GenAI capability budget. Ring-fence tcsAI platform build, reskilling waves, and the AI-solution factory. Cutting these to flatter one quarter's optics would forfeit the world's-largest-AI-led-services position — the exact trade the CEO has said he will not make.

payload.heading: How to frame it
payload.body: Frame Q1 as the seasonal wage-cycle low with GenAI investment deliberately layered in, and Q2 as the start of the recovery toward the 26% zone. Anchor on the record order book ($40.7B FY TCV) and AI revenue at $1.8B annualized as the growth proof points that justify the investment.

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: Loop in CFO Samir Seksaria (owns the bridge), the COO / Global Delivery head (utilization + pyramid), and Business-Group heads (discretionary hold). 90-minute margin war-room this week.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Lock the utilization redeployment plan.
• Confirm subcontractor substitution targets by BG.
• Publish the ring-fenced GenAI budget so no one raids it for optics.

attention_cards.title: $1.2B North America BFSI consolidation deal at final gate — pricing & ramp decision needed
attention_cards.body: A global bank is consolidating vendors; TCS is in the final two on a ~$1.2B TCV, 7-year managed-services + AI deal. Aggressive pricing and a demanding ramp raise concentration and margin questions. Client fiscal close in 3 weeks.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: Do we pursue the BFSI mega-deal as-is, reprice, or walk?

investigation.seed_question: What is the right shape for the BFSI mega-deal — pursue, reprice, or walk?
investigation.context_summary: Global bank consolidation · $1.2B TCV / 7yr · sub-22% price ask · 9-month ramp · concentration risk
investigation.recommended_action: Pursue with repriced terms — hold a 23% blended-margin floor, phase the ramp over 9 months, and lead with an AI-led productivity commitment as the differentiator. Walk only if the client will not move off the sub-22% ask.

affected_accounts.name: Incumbent vendor A — being consolidated out
Incumbent vendor B — being consolidated out

payload.heading: The economics
payload.body:
• **$1.2B TCV over 7 years** — one of the largest NA BFSI consolidation awards in play; would lift the order book and NA share meaningfully.
• **Price pressure:** the client's target implies a sub-22% blended margin in the ramp years — below the firm's ~26% and TCS's stated floor.
• **Accretion curve:** dilutive in years 1-2 during ramp, accretive from year 3 as AI-led productivity and annuity kick in.

payload.heading: Transition risk
payload.body: A 9-month cutover from two incumbents at once is demanding. Risk concentrates in data migration, regulatory approvals, and standing up the delivery pyramid without spiking subcontractor cost. The BFSI Cloud Migration (2025) playbook in Memory is the directly transferable analog — its lesson was to front-load data-quality audits.

panels.label: AI-led differentiation
panels.title: AI-Led Differentiation
panels.subtitle: Why TCS wins on more than price

payload.heading: The differentiator
payload.body: Neutralize the price fight by leading with an **AI-led productivity commitment**: contractually guarantee a ~20% run-cost reduction by year 3 via tcsAI in the SDLC and operations. This reframes the deal from rate-card to outcomes — the exact motion beating the consulting-led narrative elsewhere, and it protects blended margin while meeting the client's cost goal.

payload.heading: What to weigh
payload.body: The deal adds ~0.9pp of revenue in one account — meaningful but within guardrails given the annuity quality. Precedent: TCS has won and ramped comparable BFSI mega-deals; the discipline is to hold the margin floor rather than buy the logo. Board-level visibility given the size.

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: Deal Desk, CFO office (margin-floor sign-off), BFSI Business-Group head, and the AI-solution factory lead for the productivity commitment. Decision review before the client's fiscal close.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Model the 23%-floor repriced bid.
• Draft the AI productivity guarantee terms.
• Confirm delivery-pod availability for a 9-month ramp.

attention_cards.title: Attrition ticks to 13.8% as GenAI/cloud engineers get poached in 2 hubs — reskilling is the counter
attention_cards.body: LTM attrition rose to 13.8%, concentrated in GenAI and cloud skills at the Pune and Hyderabad hubs. The AI-fluency reskilling program is the structural counter — reskill-and-redeploy, not replace.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: How do we hold GenAI/cloud talent without a broad comp reset?

investigation.seed_question: How do we retain hot-skill talent while scaling the AI-ready workforce?
investigation.context_summary: Attrition 13.8% · GenAI/cloud 19% in Pune & Hyderabad · reskilling program at 62% · Global Top Employer
investigation.recommended_action: Approve targeted retention for the two hot-skill hubs (spot comp + accelerated growth paths) and accelerate the AI-fluency reskilling waves to lift the AI-ready workforce from 62% to 85% by Q4. Frame explicitly as skills realignment, not a cost action.

payload.heading: What the data shows
payload.body:
• **Concentration:** overall attrition (13.8%) is industry-best, but GenAI/cloud roles at Pune and Hyderabad are running ~19% as start-ups and GCCs poach.
• **Comp gap:** hot-skill comp is ~6% behind aggressive market offers, though total-rewards and growth still hold most people.
• **Not systemic:** the broad workforce is stable — this is a targeted, not a company-wide, problem.

payload.heading: The structural counter
payload.body: The tcsAI fluency program is the durable answer: AI fluency for all, GenAI developer tooling in the SDLC, and weekly AI-lab time. AI-ready workforce is at 62%, up 14pp YTD, targeting ~85% by Q4 and ~100% over time. Every reskilled engineer both lowers attrition risk and reduces subcontractor dependence — a margin and retention win at once.

payload.heading: Recommended levers
payload.body:
• **Spot comp** for the ~14,000 hot-skill cohort at the two hubs — precision, not a company-wide reset that would hit margin.
• **Accelerated growth paths:** fast-track architect and AI-lead roles for high performers.
• **Project pull:** route marquee GenAI programs to these hubs so the best work is here, not at the poachers.

payload.heading: Why culture is the moat
payload.body: The CEO's framing — the AI-first transformation is 'as much about culture as technology' — matters here. Reskill-and-redeploy protects trust; a cost-first response would spike attrition further. TCS's decade-plus Global Top Employer standing and industry-best retention are the moat competitors cannot copy quickly.

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: CHRO Milind Lakkad (owns retention + reskilling), the two hub heads, and the tcsAI program lead. Weekly hot-skill attrition tracking to the exec team.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Approve the spot-comp cohort list.
• Confirm the Q4 85% reskilling milestone plan.
• Route two marquee GenAI programs to Pune/Hyderabad.

attention_cards.title: North America discretionary softens for a 2nd quarter — 2 transformation programs paused
attention_cards.body: Two large NA clients paused discretionary transformation programs and pipeline conversion is slowing. Cost-takeout and vendor-consolidation demand is rising as an offset if freed bench is redeployed quickly.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: How do we offset the NA discretionary softness this quarter?

investigation.seed_question: What's the exposure from NA discretionary softness and how do we offset it?
investigation.context_summary: NA discretionary -6% QoQ · 2 programs paused (~$140M) · consolidation/cost-takeout pipeline +$1.8B
investigation.recommended_action: Redeploy freed bench into cost-takeout and vendor-consolidation deals, protect the FY growth narrative, and lead NA renewals with an AI-productivity story. Institute weekly demand tracking to the exec team.

payload.heading: What's happening
payload.body:
• **Discretionary pullback:** two large NA clients paused transformation programs (~$140M combined ACV) amid macro caution; a second consecutive soft quarter.
• **Not a share loss:** win rates hold — this is budget timing, not competitive loss.
• **Demand rotation:** the same caution is lifting cost-takeout and vendor-consolidation demand, which favors scaled incumbents like TCS.

payload.heading: The offset
payload.body: Client caution is a tailwind for consolidation: enterprises cutting vendor rosters route more scope to a trusted, scaled partner. TCS's consolidation and cost-takeout pipeline has grown +$1.8B. Leading with an AI-productivity story (do more for less via tcsAI) converts these faster and at defensible margin.

payload.heading: Redeploy fast
payload.body: The ~1,900 associates freed by the paused programs are a margin risk if idle and an opportunity if redeployed. Route them into the consolidation and cost-takeout wins and into reskilling waves. Fast redeployment protects utilization (85.2%) — the same lever defending Q2 margin.

payload.heading: How to frame it
payload.body: Frame the softness as macro-timing, not structural, and lead with the record $40.7B order book, the consolidation-share opportunity, and AI revenue at $1.8B. The story is: TCS converts client caution into consolidation share and productivity wins — resilient by design.

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: NA Regional head, the Business-Group heads with paused programs, and the deal desk for consolidation pursuits. Weekly demand + redeployment tracking to the exec team.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Confirm the 5 consolidation pursuits to accelerate.
• Lock bench redeployment plan (<30 days).
• Draft the AI-productivity renewal pitch for the 2 paused clients.

attention_cards.title: India sovereign-tech momentum builds — BSNL milestone, semiconductor & quantum open a new frontier
attention_cards.body: The BSNL nationwide rollout hit a major milestone, and the Tata Electronics semiconductor and IBM/Andhra Pradesh quantum programs are maturing. A distinctive India-led, sovereign-tech growth engine to frame for the board.
attention_cards.chat_seed_question: How do we frame the India sovereign-tech growth engine for the board?

investigation.seed_question: How large and how fundable is the India sovereign-tech growth engine?
investigation.context_summary: BSNL $1.0B+ milestone · semiconductor (Tata Electronics) · quantum (IBM + Andhra Pradesh) · sovereign pipeline +$2.5B
investigation.recommended_action: Frame the India sovereign-tech engine at the July board — BSNL as proof, semiconductor and quantum as option value — and approve targeted capability investment. Position it as a differentiated, India-led growth story that peers cannot easily replicate.

payload.heading: Why BSNL matters
payload.body:
• **Proof at scale:** the BSNL nationwide network program (a $1.0B+ mandate) demonstrates TCS can deliver sovereign, nation-scale technology end-to-end.
• **Reference value:** it anchors credibility for further government, PSU, and sovereign-cloud pursuits.
• **Indigenous story:** it aligns with India's self-reliance agenda — a policy tailwind peers with less local depth cannot match.

payload.heading: The deep-tech options
payload.body:
• **Semiconductor:** chip-design work with Tata Electronics extends TCS into a high-value, scarce-skill frontier.
• **Quantum:** the IBM + Government of Andhra Pradesh quantum hub positions TCS at the leading edge of a nascent, high-optionality market.
These are capability bets ahead of revenue — classic Transformation-Driver moves that build differentiated moats.

payload.heading: How to frame it
payload.body: Position for the July board as a **differentiated, India-led growth engine**: BSNL as delivered proof, semiconductor and quantum as option value, and sovereign cloud as the near-term pipeline (+$2.5B). The ask: targeted capability investment now, framed as building the moat while peers chase the same enterprise AI budgets.

payload.heading: Why it compounds
payload.body: Sovereign and indigenous-tech work is sticky (multi-year, mission-critical), reference-rich (each win pulls the next), and skill-scarce (hard for peers to staff). It diversifies TCS's growth away from discretionary enterprise cycles — exactly the resilience the CEO emphasizes.

payload.heading: Suggested working group
payload.body: The Chief Strategy Officer, the India/Regional Markets head, and the deep-tech (semiconductor/quantum) leads. Prepare the board narrative and the capability-investment ask.

payload.heading: First 24 hours
payload.body:
• Finalize the BSNL milestone proof points.
• Size the sovereign-cloud pipeline for the board.
• Draft the capability-investment ask for chip + quantum.

signals.title: Infosys Topaz undercuts TCS on price in mid-market GenAI
signals.body: Infosys Topaz targets mid-market with aggressive fixed-price GenAI bundles ~15% below TCS custom pricing; reinforced at Q2 FY26 earnings.
signals.recommended_action: Ship a productized mid-market GenAI SKU to remove price as the deciding factor.

detail.why_it_matters: Mid-market is TCS's fastest-growing GenAI segment; a price war there compresses margin fastest.
detail.key_insight: Price has become the mid-market battleground, not capability.
detail.confidence_description: Corroborated by two independent field reports and Infosys launch/earnings materials.

detail.key_insight: The gap is narrative and outcome-proof, not capability or cost.

signals.title: US $100,000 H-1B petition fee reshapes onshore delivery economics
signals.body: The US proclamation imposing a $100,000 H-1B petition fee raises onshore staffing cost and accelerates the shift to nearshore and local hiring.
signals.recommended_action: Accelerate US local hiring and nearshore (LATAM) build-out to de-risk delivery cost.

detail.why_it_matters: Onshore cost inflation directly pressures margin on US-delivered work and changes bid economics.
detail.key_insight: Delivery mix — not visa strategy alone — is now the lever that protects US margin.
detail.confidence_description: Based on the published federal proclamation and legal-counsel interpretation.

takeaways.action: Scale US local + LATAM nearshore hiring

signals.recommended_action: Deploy targeted spot-comp and accelerated reskilling to protect scarce GenAI/cloud talent.

detail.why_it_matters: Scarce-skill attrition directly caps the GenAI delivery ramp and inflates subcontractor cost.

How fast can we package CX use cases?

signals.title: Cybersecurity mega-demand rises in energy and BFSI
signals.body: Escalating threat activity lifts cybersecurity and AI-security demand across energy/utilities and BFSI in North America and the Middle East.
signals.recommended_action: Bundle AI-augmented security operations into GenAI and cloud deals as a cross-sell.

detail.why_it_matters: Cybersecurity is the #1 CIO priority and a durable, cross-sellable annuity attached to every deal.
detail.key_insight: AI-augmented SOC is where demand and differentiation now concentrate.

questions.text: Which deals can attach AI-SOC now?
What SLAs can we commit?
Where is demand most acute regionally?
What references prove AI-SOC value?

signals.title: Competitive leadership moves and mid-tier alliances reshape the field
signals.body: A rival poaches a senior GenAI delivery leader while an LTIMindtree–IBM alliance and a peer divestiture reshape mid-market competition.
signals.recommended_action: Retain key GenAI leaders and monitor alliance moves that could shift mid-market dynamics.

detail.why_it_matters: Leadership and alliance shifts move delivery capability and mid-market share faster than organic growth.
detail.key_insight: Talent stability and ecosystem alliances are competitive moats, not HR footnotes.

questions.text: Which leaders are highest flight-risk?
How does the alliance change mid-market?
What capability gap opens if they leave?
What is the counter-retention offer?

memory_items.title: GenAI Go-To-Market Ramp — Top 50 Accounts
memory_items.body: Consulting-led GenAI motion targeting top 50 accounts with outcome-based pricing, mobilized after the Accenture bookings gap.

nodes.label: Options: SWAT / Outcome SKU / Reference velocity
K Krithivasan Selected: Option A
nodes.sub: GenAI SWAT pods routed to 3 accounts

logs.change: SWAT + outcome pricing modeled at +11pp win
Option A selected — SWAT pods routed

memory_items.title: BFSI Cloud Migration Program (2025)
memory_items.body: Completed 40-account BFSI cloud migration ahead of schedule; became the reference motion for the GenAI ramp.

learnings.body: Dedicated migration SWAT pods cut cutover time 30%.
Underestimated legacy data-cleansing effort in Q1; caused a 3-week slip mid-program.
Front-load data-quality audits before committing account timelines.

memory_items.title: Q2 Margin-Defense Lever Program — FY26
memory_items.body: Coordinated lever program to defend Q2 operating margin against the wage-cycle and GenAI-investment drag, without cutting the AI capability budget.

nodes.label: External: Wage-cycle norm
nodes.sub: Peers absorbing similar Q1 wage drag

memory_items.title: AI-Ready Workforce & GenAI Reskilling Wave
memory_items.body: Enterprise reskilling program to lift AI-ready workforce from 62% to 85% by Q4 and counter GenAI/cloud attrition through reskill-and-redeploy, not replacement.

nodes.label: Companion Correlates
nodes.sub: Reskilling widens supply; retention alone = bidding war

memory_items.title: NA BFSI Mega-Deal Pursuit — $1.2B Consolidation
memory_items.body: Final-gate pursuit of a $1.2B, 7-year North America BFSI consolidation deal; repriced to a 23% margin floor and differentiated with an AI-led productivity commitment.

nodes.label: External: Client vendor consolidation
nodes.sub: cutting roster to a scaled partner

nodes.label: Companion Correlates
nodes.sub: Accretive yr3+; AI productivity reframes price

nodes.label: Options: As-is / Reprice / Walk
nodes.sub: B: reprice to 23% floor + AI commitment

nodes.label: K Krithivasan Selected: Reprice + AI Commitment

memory_items.title: GenAI Pilot-to-Scale Playbook — Global Insurer (2025)
memory_items.body: Converted a stalled GenAI claims-automation pilot at a global insurer into an enterprise-scale program with outcome-based pricing — the analog now fueling the Top-50 GenAI ramp.

nodes.label: External: Board pressure for AI ROI
Companion Correlates
nodes.sub: Scale-path + outcome pricing unlock the mandate

learnings.body: Leading with a fixed-outcome first use case neutralized the competitor's consulting framing and unlocked the enterprise mandate.
The initial pilot lingered too long in evaluation; delay let a competitor re-enter and nearly cost the account.
Set a pilot-to-scale decision gate at 90 days with a pre-agreed outcome-pricing path, so pilots convert before competitors circle.

prompts.template: You are a strategic assistant for {firm_name}. The CXO is {cxo_name}, {cxo_title}. Ask clarifying questions first.
Provide deep strategic analysis for {firm_name} using data-driven insights.
You are a simulation engine for {firm_name}. Run quick what-if scenarios with quantified impact, assumptions, and confidence levels.

wargame_competitors.name: Accenture
wargame_competitors.positioning: Consulting-led GenAI leader. Reinvention narrative winning C-suite mandates in BFSI and Products.

scenarios.title: Accenture wins the 3 contested top-20 GenAI RFPs
scenarios.impact_assessment: Swings ~$2.1B of pipeline and widens the GenAI bookings gap toward ~$2B, pressuring TCS's growth narrative with analysts.
scenarios.recommended_response: Deploy GenAI SWAT pods within 48h, lead with outcome-based pricing, and fast-track two closed BFSI references.

scenarios.title: Accenture holds premium and cedes mid-market on price
scenarios.impact_assessment: Opens room for a TCS productized GenAI SKU to take mid-market share while Accenture defends enterprise margin.
scenarios.recommended_response: Ship the mid-market GenAI bundle ~15% below custom to capture the segment Accenture won't chase.

wargame_competitors.name: Infosys
wargame_competitors.positioning: Closest Indian-heritage rival. Topaz (AI) + Cobalt (cloud); aggressive on price and AI narrative.
weaknesses: Narrower domain depth in some verticals
recent_moves: Launched Topaz mid-market GenAI bundles ~15% below TCS custom

scenarios.title: Infosys triggers a mid-market GenAI price war
scenarios.impact_assessment: Compresses mid-market margin fastest — TCS's fastest-growing GenAI segment — and stalls evaluations on price.
scenarios.recommended_response: Ship a productized mid-market GenAI SKU to remove price as the deciding factor; hold a 24% margin floor.

scenarios.title: Infosys wins on speed-to-productize in a key account
scenarios.impact_assessment: Cedes a reference logo and momentum in the productized-GenAI narrative.
scenarios.recommended_response: Counter with TCS's domain depth + sovereign/reference proof and an outcome guarantee.

wargame_competitors.name: Cognizant
wargame_competitors.positioning: US-heavy; strong in healthcare and BFSI; regaining large-deal momentum with Neuro AI.
strengths: Healthcare & BFSI depth in the US

scenarios.title: Cognizant wins US healthcare GenAI mandates
scenarios.impact_assessment: Erodes TCS's US healthcare growth lane where Cognizant is strongest.
scenarios.recommended_response: Lead with TCS Life-Sciences/ADD depth and cross-industry AI references; protect key US healthcare accounts.

scenarios.title: Cognizant competes on US onshore amid visa cost rise
scenarios.impact_assessment: US-heavy delivery gives Cognizant a localization edge as H-1B costs rise.
scenarios.recommended_response: Accelerate US local hiring and near-shore; differentiate on AI-led productivity, not headcount.
